creator: olgeni@FreeBSD.org
text:
When building Erlang R15B02 on FreeBSD 9.1-PRERELEASE amd64, with
gcc version 4.2.1 20070831 and DTRACE support enabled, the compiler
will reach an infinite loop when compiling erl_nif.c.

Using gcc 4.6.3 (lang/gcc) will work as expected.

Fix: 

None known so far, only the gcc 4.6.3 workaround.
How-To-Repeat: 
- Enable DTrace support on your system; in particular, add the
  following lines to make.conf:

    WITH_CTF=1
    STRIP=
    CFLAGS+=-fno-omit-frame-pointer

- Enable the DTRACE option for lang/erlang, disable the GCC46 option
  and try to rebuild. You will have to explicitly disable the IGNORE
  line in the Makefile preventing this specific configuration.

- Start the build and it will eventually reach this point:

[...]
cc -Iamd64-portbld-freebsd9.1/opt/smp -Ibeam -Isys/unix -Isys/common -Iamd64-portbld-freebsd9.1 -Izlib  -Ipcre -Ihipe -I../include -I../include/amd64-portbld-freebsd9.1 -I../include/internal -I../include/internal/amd64-portbld-freebsd9.1   -O3 -fomit-frame-pointer -pipe -fno-omit-frame-pointer -fno-strict-aliasing -std=gnu89 -I/usr/ports/lang/erlang/work/otp_src_R15B02/erts/amd64-portbld-freebsd9.1   -DERTS_SMP -DHAVE_CONFIG_H -Wall -Wstrict-prototypes -Wmissing-prototypes -Wdeclaration-after-statement -DUSE_THREADS -D_THREAD_SAFE -D_REENTRANT -DPOSIX_THREADS  -c beam/safe_hash.c -o obj/amd64-portbld-freebsd9.1/opt/smp/safe_hash.o
cc -Iamd64-portbld-freebsd9.1/opt/smp -Ibeam -Isys/unix -Isys/common -Iamd64-portbld-freebsd9.1 -Izlib  -Ipcre -Ihipe -I../include -I../include/amd64-portbld-freebsd9.1 -I../include/internal -I../include/internal/amd64-portbld-freebsd9.1   -O3 -fomit-frame-pointer -pipe -fno-omit-frame-pointer -fno-strict-aliasing -std=gnu89 -I/usr/ports/lang/erlang/work/otp_src_R15B02/erts/amd64-portbld-freebsd9.1   -DERTS_SMP -DHAVE_CONFIG_H -Wall -Wstrict-prototypes -Wmissing-prototypes -Wdeclaration-after-statement -DUSE_THREADS -D_THREAD_SAFE -D_REENTRANT -DPOSIX_THREADS  -c beam/erl_zlib.c -o obj/amd64-portbld-freebsd9.1/opt/smp/erl_zlib.o
cc -Iamd64-portbld-freebsd9.1/opt/smp -Ibeam -Isys/unix -Isys/common -Iamd64-portbld-freebsd9.1 -Izlib  -Ipcre -Ihipe -I../include -I../include/amd64-portbld-freebsd9.1 -I../include/internal -I../include/internal/amd64-portbld-freebsd9.1   -O3 -fomit-frame-pointer -pipe -fno-omit-frame-pointer -fno-strict-aliasing -std=gnu89 -I/usr/ports/lang/erlang/work/otp_src_R15B02/erts/amd64-portbld-freebsd9.1   -DERTS_SMP -DHAVE_CONFIG_H -Wall -Wstrict-prototypes -Wmissing-prototypes -Wdeclaration-after-statement -DUSE_THREADS -D_THREAD_SAFE -D_REENTRANT -DPOSIX_THREADS  -c beam/erl_nif.c -o obj/amd64-portbld-freebsd9.1/opt/smp/erl_nif.o
beam/erl_nif.c: In function 'load_nif_2':
beam/erl_nif.c:1587: warning: passing argument 1 of 'erts_is_atom_str' discards qualifiers from pointer target type

[here it will loop indefinitely with CPU load at 100%]

This does not happen on i386.
